questions.prompt: A political advertisement uses emotional music and attractive actors but presents almost no policy information. Viewers report liking the candidate but cannot explain why. Which persuasion process best explains this?
A student reads several detailed research articles before changing their opinion about climate policy. The change persists for months. Which persuasion route most likely produced this change?

A charity first asks people to sign a small pledge supporting clean water initiatives. Weeks later they ask those same people for a large donation. Many donate. Which persuasion technique explains this?
A salesperson first asks a customer to buy a very expensive product. When the customer refuses, they offer a much cheaper alternative, which the customer accepts. Which persuasion technique is this?
An advertisement claims that 'millions of people already use this product.' Consumers assume it must be good. Which persuasion principle is this?

A doctor recommends a medication while wearing a white lab coat and using technical language. Patients trust the recommendation largely because of the doctor's professional status. Which persuasion principle explains this?
A store advertises 'last chance — sale ends tonight!' even though the product will still be available later. Which persuasion principle is being used?

A student agrees with the group’s incorrect answer about a math problem because everyone else in the group chose that answer and the student assumes they must know something they don't. Which influence is operating?
A person laughs at a joke they did not understand because everyone else laughed and they want to fit in socially. Which influence explains this behavior?
In a line judgment experiment, participants knowingly choose incorrect answers after hearing others do so. Which classic study does this resemble?
Participants administer shocks to another person because an experimenter insists they continue. Which foundational research study does this replicate?
A person deliberately chooses the opposite of what a group suggests because they strongly value personal freedom. Which concept best explains this?
A skilled musician performs better in front of an audience when playing a familiar piece but performs worse when trying a new complex composition. Which theory explains this pattern?
A worker contributes less effort during a group project because individual contributions are not evaluated separately. Which concept explains this behavior?
A group assignment improves when each member is responsible for a clearly defined section of the project. Which problem is being reduced?
Fans in a large masked crowd begin rioting after a sports loss. Many later say they felt anonymous and not personally responsible. Which theory explains this behavior?
A discussion group about politics becomes more extreme after members discuss their shared views. Which phenomenon explains this change?
A group of executives refuses to question a risky decision because they want to maintain harmony within the group. Which process best explains this?
Members of a committee privately doubt a decision but stay silent to avoid conflict. Which groupthink symptom is this?
A team member blocks negative information from reaching the group leader to maintain consensus. Which groupthink symptom is this?
A decision-making team assigns someone to challenge every proposal and question assumptions. Which strategy is being used?
A person helps a stranger even though they expect no reward and no one will know they helped. Which concept best describes this?
Someone volunteers at a hospital because helping others improves their mood. Which explanation fits best?
A person donates money after feeling guilty for ignoring a previous request. Which explanation best fits?
People are less likely to help someone having a seizure in a crowded subway station because they assume someone else will help. What phenomenon explains this?
A person helps their sibling financially but refuses to help a stranger in the same situation. Which theory explains this difference?
A violent attack occurs because the attacker wants revenge and is driven by anger. Which type of aggression is this?
A person robs a store and threatens violence only to obtain money. Which type of aggression is this?
Children imitate aggressive behaviors they observe adults performing toward toys in an experiment. Which theory explains this?
A person insults a coworker after being yelled at by their boss earlier in the day. Which aggression theory explains redirecting anger to a safer target?
Programs teaching children emotional regulation, empathy, and conflict resolution aim to reduce aggression before it occurs. Which prevention level is this?
Programs encouraging men to challenge harmful gender norms and promote healthy masculinity are designed to reduce which problem?
